reason: Release Notes v1.0.0 is comprehensive and internally consistent: §1-§2 describe Gate 3→Gate 4 commits (injection_guard extraction as root-cause fix, trace regen, DA context) with no behaviour changes; §3 enumerates all 5 FRs with Gate-1 scores that exactly match quality_manifest.json::gate_results.gate1 (96.94/94.22/99.66/99.36/95.92) and module mappings that match fr_module_traceability; §4 NFR table covers all 6 NFRs with verdicts consistent with 05-verification/VERIFICATION_REPORT.md §3; §5 Gate 4 composite (91.92) matches .sessi-work/gate4_result.json::composite_score and is above the 85 threshold; §6 test/coverage snapshot (175 cases PASS in 11.03s, 100% src / 94% combined) matches VERIFICATION_REPORT.md §3-§4 and BASELINE.md §3-§4; §7 security (bandit 0 high/0 medium/2 low informational; gitleaks clean; shell=True 0 hits) matches VERIFICATION_REPORT.md §7; §8 known limitations are honest (architecture=60 da_waiver, run_task CC=14, breaker MI=66.92, no pytest-benchmark, mutation_testing disabled, .bak files); §9 verification provenance table is exhaustive.

gaps.message: Composite score 91.92 is cited with explicit reference to .sessi-work/gate4_result.json (RELEASE_NOTES.md:69). However, .methodology/gate4_result.json still carries 90.2222 (the agent-raw value before harness_cli.py:3201 patches the persistent copy on finalize). Both numbers are ≥85 release threshold — not a blocker — but downstream CI / PhaseAuditor C10 reads .methodology/gate4_result.json, so the deliverable should either (a) cite both numbers with a note that the persistent file is the canonical CI artefact, or (b) recommend re-running finalize-gate --gate 4 --phase 6 to resync.

§8 limitation #6 mentions legacy .bak files (breaker.py.bak, cache.py.bak, cli.py.bak) in 03-development/src/taskq/ — same advisory appears in 05-verification/VERIFICATION_REPORT.md §8.2 and BASELINE.md §2 note. Recommend filing a housekeeping ticket for a future release; not blocking.
